# Claim/Evidence Matrix

This initial matrix contains one core placeholder claim per chapter.

No claim is marked `source-derived`, `prototype-backed`, `synthetic-test-backed`, `empirical-test-backed`, or `external-literature-backed` yet. Those labels require source ingestion, prototype inspection, or actual test execution.

| Claim ID | Chapter | Claim | Current support state | Assigned sources | Current evidence | Open gap |
|---|---:|---|---|---|---|---|
| `01-core` | 01 | Efficient ASI should be modeled as a governed stack of cooperating layers rather than as a single undifferentiated model. | argument | `viea`, `beastbrain`, `aletheia`, `moecot`, `talos` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `02-core` | 02 | Efficient ASI is a design hypothesis: capability improves when cognition is routed, compressed, reused, governed, and tested. | argument | `viea`, `moecot`, `rmi`, `cgs`, `rankfold_neuralfold`, `bbvca_v9`, `simulation_scaling`, `beastbrain`, `beastbrain_timeless`, `aletheia` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `03-core` | 03 | Ungoverned intelligence fails through stack-level breakdowns before any dramatic science-fiction failure mode is needed. | argument | `scf`, `vcm_public`, `talos`, `spinoza`, `field_of_god`, `viea`, `simulation_scaling` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `04-core` | 04 | The alignment layer should act as a constitutional substrate that constrains goals, plans, and self-modification. | argument | `alignment_field`, `field_of_god`, `ethica_mechanica`, `eternal_code`, `coherence_exchange` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `05-core` | 05 | Governed self-improvement requires stable capability fields: stable semantic boundaries with replaceable implementations and bounded authority. | argument | `scf`, `talos`, `ladon_manhattan`, `viea`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `06-core` | 06 | Planning is a separate control layer that converts goals into governed action without owning memory, reasoning, or side effects. | argument | `planforge`, `planforge_compiler_arch`, `cognitive_compilation`, `viea`, `software_magic_grimoire`, `moecot` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `07-core` | 07 | Virtual Context Memory should compile active context packets from durable memory using evidence, authority labels, and planner requests. | argument | `vcm_public`, `vcm_editable`, `context_engineer`, `black_hole_context_manager`, `verification_bandwidth`, `moecot`, `viea` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `08-core` | 08 | The execution layer should convert plans into typed jobs and artifacts through a governed labor operating system. | argument | `talos`, `talos_md`, `viea`, `genesiscode`, `software_magic_grimoire`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `moecot`, `spinoza_composer`, `ladon_manhattan` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `09-core` | 09 | Reasoning and verification should maintain claim records with evidence tiers, contradiction handling, and calibrated uncertainty. | argument | `spinoza`, `uat`, `coherence_exchange`, `verification_bandwidth`, `treellm`, `viea`, `spinoza_composer`, `aletheia` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `10-core` | 10 | ASI scales by routing across bounded specialist modules with explicit readiness gates and benchmark-driven promotion. | argument | `octopus_router`, `rmi`, `moecot`, `beastbrain`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `benchmaxxing`, `moecot_md`, `rgs` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `11-core` | 11 | Compression is a first-class stack layer only when it exposes residual burden, fallback paths, and downstream utility tests. | argument | `cgs`, `rgs`, `rankfold_neuralfold`, `rankfold_compressor`, `bbvca_v9`, `bbvca_main`, `bugbrain`, `simulation_scaling`, `treellm` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `12-core` | 12 | Alternative substrates belong in the stack only as testable implementation hypotheses until evidence supports them. | argument | `coilmoecot`, `temporal_coil_research`, `cognitive_compilation`, `genesiscode`, `viea` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `13-core` | 13 | The book should maintain evidence ratchets that connect claims, tests, residuals, regressions, and release history. | argument | `benchmaxxing`, `moecot`, `rmi`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `road_to_agi`, `viea`, `tokenmana`, `coherence_exchange`, `uat` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `14-core` | 14 | The stack can be specified as an integrated reference architecture with typed handoffs between layers. | argument | `viea`, `moecot`, `scf`, `vcm_public`, `planforge`, `talos`, `spinoza`, `alignment_field`, `octopus_router`, `rmi`, `benchmaxxing` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `15-core` | 15 | The prototype should grow in staged increments from artifact graph to governed capability replacement. | argument | `moecot`, `viea`, `road_to_agi`, `benchmaxxing`, `scf`, `vcm_public`, `planforge`, `beastbrain`, `moecot_md`, `coherence_exchange`, `beastbrain_timeless` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |
| `16-core` | 16 | The living book should model the ASI stack discipline through Quarto source, inventories, evidence matrices, tests, changelogs, and releases. | argument | `road_to_agi`, `benchmaxxing`, `viea`, `moecot`, `moecot_md`, `bugbrain` | Outline-level architecture claim only. | Ingest sources; define and run tests before raising support state. |

## Support States

| State | Meaning |
|---|---|
| unsupported | Not yet supported; likely remove or mark as speculative. |
| argument | Supported by reasoning only. |
| source-derived | Derived from supplied source papers. |
| prototype-backed | Implemented in a prototype but not robustly tested. |
| synthetic-test-backed | Supported by controlled synthetic tests. |
| empirical-test-backed | Supported by external or realistic tests. |
| external-literature-backed | Supported by third-party literature. |
